fgr

Describe a factory as a little graph — get a verified, paste-and-run Factorio blueprint.

You write what to build (machines and the lanes between them). The compiler works out where everything goes — belts, inserters, undergrounds, pipes, loaders, even the power grid — and an independent verifier traces every item and fluid through the placed tiles to prove the layout really does what you asked.

155/155
tracked cases verify
(49 curated + 106 stress)
paste & run
power grid + EEI included
works in sandbox as-is
full-belt I/O
chests couple via loaders
both lanes, no bottleneck
1 oracle
3 swappable generators
the verifier is the judge

Thirty seconds of how

.fgr DSL ──parse──▶ Graph(spec) ──compile──▶ Layout ──verify──▶ PASS/FAIL ──▶ blueprint string

Lanes: A -> B belt · A -> B, C one belt, one tap per consumer (no splitters) · A, B -> C multi-tap merge · A ~> B fluid pipe. The compiler (v3) routes every lane as a negotiated multi-terminal net — PathFinder-style congestion pricing, the same idea FPGAs route with.

A PASS is physical, not claimed: no overlaps, every declared lane connects (and none you didn't declare), no two products share a belt lane, fluids stay in isolated networks attached at real fluid-box tiles, and every machine sits on a live, wired power network. If it passes, it runs.

bus PASS

The signature move: one belt feeds three consumers — each taps the passing belt with its own inserter. No splitters, no spaghetti.

6 machines · 5 lanes · 56 entities placed · compiled in 4 ms
⏱ expected throughput (also embedded in the blueprint's in-game tooltip)
out_gears: up to 1.5/s alone, 1.5/s with all outputs running, ~0.469/s sustained through the placed hardware
out_sticks: up to 3.0/s alone, 1.5/s with all outputs running, ~0.469/s sustained through the placed hardware
bottleneck: gears (iron-gear-wheel) at 1.5 crafts/s
link near capacity: iron->gears at 320% (1 inserter arm(s))
steady-state estimate from Factorio prototype data; see docs/RATES.md
show the DSL source 
# One belt feeding several consumers, tapped by inline inserters (NO splitters).
#
#                         .-> assembler gears   -> out_gears
#   iron --(one belt)----+--> assembler sticks  -> out_sticks
#                         '-> output  out_raw
#
# `iron -> gears, sticks, out_raw` is a SINGLE output belt off the iron chest; each
# consumer taps it with its own inserter where the belt passes (the canonical "one
# belt feeds many machines" pattern — no splitter gadget). Contrast with three
# separate `iron -> ...` lines, which would demand three output inserters on the
# (1x1) iron chest — impossible. The shared belt needs only one.

input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler gears   : iron-gear-wheel
assembler sticks  : iron-stick
output    out_gears
output    out_sticks
output    out_raw

iron  -> gears, sticks, out_raw
gears  -> out_gears
sticks -> out_sticks
[ok] all 8 verifier checks pass · recipes + ingredients match Factorio's own data — expand
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] no item mixing on a belt lane (max two products per belt, one per side)
[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

circuits PASS

Two ingredient lanes converge on one assembler. The compiler gives it two input inserters and routes both belts without a crossing.

5 machines · 4 lanes · 49 entities placed · compiled in 5 ms
⏱ expected throughput (also embedded in the blueprint's in-game tooltip)
chips: up to 1.0/s alone, 1.0/s with all outputs running, ~0.3127/s sustained through the placed hardware
bottleneck: cable (copper-cable) at 1.5 crafts/s
link near capacity: cable->circuit at 320% (1 inserter arm(s))
link near capacity: copper->cable at 160% (1 inserter arm(s))
link near capacity: iron->circuit at 106% (1 inserter arm(s))
steady-state estimate from Factorio prototype data; see docs/RATES.md
show the DSL source 
# A two-stage factory with a multi-input assembler.
#
# copper-plate -> copper-cable -.
#                                +-> electronic-circuit -> output
# iron-plate -------------------'
#
# `circuit` pulls TWO lanes (iron + cable); the compiler must give it two input
# inserters on its west side and route both belt lanes without collisions. The
# iron lane also has to travel PAST the cable column to reach the circuit column.

input     copper  : copper-plate
input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler cable   : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
output    chips

copper -> cable
cable  -> circuit
iron   -> circuit
circuit -> chips

processing_unit PASS

Reconvergent electronics (cable feeds green and red; green feeds red and blue) plus sulfuric acid piped in — the pipe tunnels under the belt field into the assembler's real fluid box.

9 machines · 10 lanes · 161 entities placed · compiled in 41 ms
⏱ expected throughput (also embedded in the blueprint's in-game tooltip)
chips: up to 0.0375/s alone, 0.0375/s with all outputs running, ~0.013/s sustained through the placed hardware
bottleneck: cable (copper-cable) at 1.5 crafts/s
link near capacity: cable->green at 288% (1 inserter arm(s))
link near capacity: copper->cable at 160% (1 inserter arm(s))
link near capacity: iron->green at 96% (1 inserter arm(s))
steady-state estimate from Factorio prototype data; see docs/RATES.md
show the DSL source 
# Deep electronics: copper-plate -> cable -> green -> red -> blue (processing-unit).
# Reconvergent (cable->green,red; green->red,blue). Sulfuric acid is a FLUID (~>).
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input plastic : plastic-bar
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler blue : processing-unit   # processing-unit needs sulfuric-acid (crafting-with-fluid) -> assembler
output chips
copper -> cable
cable -> green, red
iron -> green
green -> red, blue
plastic -> red
red -> blue
acid ~> blue
blue -> chips

flying_robot_frame PASS

A deep multi-step build: smelting, gears, engines, batteries — with two different fluids (lubricant, acid) kept in isolated pipe networks.

14 machines · 20 lanes · 363 entities placed · compiled in 51 ms
⏱ expected throughput (also embedded in the blueprint's in-game tooltip)
frame_out: up to 0.0375/s alone, 0.0375/s with all outputs running
bottleneck: frame (flying-robot-frame) at 0.0375 crafts/s
steady-state estimate from Factorio prototype data; see docs/RATES.md
show the DSL source 
# Deep multi-step with smelting (furnace) and fluids (pipes): flying-robot-frame.
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
fluid lube : lubricant
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
furnace steel : steel-plate
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit   # crafting-with-fluid (lubricant) -> assembler, not chemical
chemical battery : battery                  # battery is a chemistry recipe -> chemical plant
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
output frame_out
iron -> steel, gear, pipe_part, green, battery
copper -> cable, battery
cable -> green
gear -> engine
pipe_part -> engine
steel -> engine, frame
engine -> eengine
green -> eengine, frame
lube ~> eengine
acid ~> battery
eengine -> frame
battery -> frame
frame -> frame_out

science_3 PASS

A red + green + military science mall: smelting, ammo and grenade lines, a coal/iron merge — every machine fed its real ingredients, end to end.

21 machines · 28 lanes · 598 entities placed · compiled in 75 ms
⏱ expected throughput (also embedded in the blueprint's in-game tooltip)
green_out: up to 0.125/s alone, 0.125/s with all outputs running, ~0.0938/s sustained through the placed hardware
mil_out: up to 0.125/s alone, 0.125/s with all outputs running, ~0.0938/s sustained through the placed hardware
red_out: up to 0.15/s alone, 0.125/s with all outputs running, ~0.0938/s sustained through the placed hardware
bottleneck: brick (stone-brick) at 0.625 crafts/s
link near capacity: stone->brick at 133% (1 inserter arm(s))
steady-state estimate from Factorio prototype data; see docs/RATES.md
show the DSL source 
# science_3: red + green + military science mall, furnace-heavy (steel + brick),
# using a merge (coal + iron -> grenade). No fluids; all items on belts.
#   red      = automation-science-pack = copper-plate + iron-gear-wheel
#   green    = logistic-science-pack   = inserter + transport-belt
#   military = military-science-pack    = piercing-rounds-magazine + grenade + stone-wall

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green_chip : electronic-circuit
assembler arm : inserter
assembler belt : transport-belt
assembler firearm_mag : firearm-magazine
assembler pierce : piercing-rounds-magazine
assembler grenade : grenade
assembler wall : stone-wall
assembler redsci : automation-science-pack
assembler greensci : logistic-science-pack
assembler milsci : military-science-pack

output red_out
output green_out
output mil_out

# raw distribution
iron -> gear, green_chip, arm, belt, steel, firearm_mag
copper -> cable, redsci, pierce
stone -> brick

# merge: two belts feed the grenade line
coal, iron -> grenade

# smelting
steel -> pierce
brick -> wall

# circuits / movement
cable -> green_chip
green_chip -> arm
gear -> arm, belt, redsci

# ammo line
firearm_mag -> pierce

# science assembly
pierce -> milsci
grenade -> milsci
wall -> milsci
arm -> greensci
belt -> greensci

redsci -> red_out
greensci -> green_out
milsci -> mil_out

[ok ] every powered entity is on a powered network (substations + EEI)

Give it a rate — the solver sizes the factory

Annotate an input (@ 1 belt) or an output (@ 0.5/s) and fgr solve derives machine counts, inserter arms and the number of input belts — then the layout is compiled, verified, and measured in the real game (headless). Every machine inside is vanilla-buildable; the boundary scaffolding (chest + loader belts, power feed) is what you replace when splicing into a real base.

input copper : copper-plate
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
chemical battery : battery
output out @ 0.5/s

iron -> battery
copper -> battery
acid ~> battery
battery -> out

circuits_1ps PASS

OUTPUT-driven, two stages: 1 electronic circuit / second. Circuits eat 3 cables per craft, so both stages go multi-copy and each circuit machine gets its own dedicated cable lane — multi-arm feeding expressed as separate same-product lanes.

show the DSL source 
# OUTPUT-DRIVEN, two stages: 1 electronic circuit per second.
# Circuits eat 3 cables/craft -> two cable arms per circuit machine; both stages
# also run 2 output arms where the output rate demands it.
input copper : copper-plate
input iron : iron-plate
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
output chips @ 1/s

copper -> cable
cable -> circuit
iron -> circuit
circuit -> chips

gears_belt PASS

INPUT-driven: one belt of iron in — max gears out. A 5-machine bank row, each machine fed by THREE iron arms and drained by TWO output arms, planned to 92% of the belt so no machine permanently starves. Measured 6.72/s with the first gear at 19 seconds — 94 entities.

3 machines · 2 lanes · 94 entities placed · compiled in 2 ms

show the DSL source 
# INPUT-DRIVEN: one belt of iron in -> as many gears as that supply allows.
# The solver sizes 5 machines (3 iron arms in + 2 output arms each), planned to
# 92% of the belt so no machine permanently starves; game-measured 6.75 gears/s.
input iron : iron-plate @ 1 belt
assembler gears : iron-gear-wheel
output out

iron -> gears
gears -> out

greenchips_belt PASS

THE SCALE TEST: a full yellow belt (15/s) of electronic circuits from an eight-line spec, compiled by the BANK generator: sandwich rows with belts as local buses, machines positioned by prefix demand, and a lane WEAVE at the exit (a merge that fills BOTH belt sides — splitters preserve lane sides, so the second collector tunnels under and side-loads from the north). The boundary is minimal too: one iron belt (a splitter halves it across the two blocks) plus two copper belts (22.5/s is physics). 649 entities (was 12,000 routed point-to-point), measured 14.43/s steady, first circuit at 36 seconds, zero idle machines.

5 machines · 4 lanes · 649 entities placed · compiled in 5 ms

# THE BIG ONE: a full yellow belt (15/s) of electronic circuits.
# 45 machines via multi-inserter feeds: 3 cable arms per circuit machine, 2 output
# ports per cable machine; two copper belts + two iron belts in (appetite-sized).
input copper : copper-plate
input iron : iron-plate
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
output chips @ 15/s

copper -> cable
cable -> circuit
iron -> circuit
circuit -> chips

greensci_05 PASS

DEEP CHAIN, six stages: 0.5 green science / second — shared iron, reconvergent gears. The honest one: measured throughput lands at ~66% of plan because multi-column nets still route interior tap arms (splitter support is the tracked fix). The pipeline catches its own gaps.

# DEEP CHAIN: 0.5 green science/s -- six production stages, shared iron,
# reconvergent intermediates (gears feed both inserters and belts).
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler arm : inserter
assembler belt : transport-belt
assembler green : logistic-science-pack
output out @ 0.5/s

copper -> cable
cable -> circuit
iron -> circuit, gear, arm, belt
gear -> arm, belt
circuit -> arm
arm -> green
belt -> green
green -> out

redsci_15 PASS

OUTPUT-driven at scale: 1.5 red science / second. As a bank: gear machines positioned by PREFIX DEMAND along the bus (a producer placed after its consumers starves them — the positional physics, handled at placement). Measured 1.65/s — exactly the predicted equilibrium — with the first pack at 40 seconds; the routed version was still ramping toward it after 40 minutes.

Trust, but verify the verifier

The oracle's model of the game (inserter pickup/drop sides, fluid-box tiles, footprints, tunnel pairing) is itself checked against Factorio's real prototype data via FBSR:

validate-model — all checks
[ok ] inserter picks at +direction, drops at -direction (all orientations) — Factorio base pickup=[0, -1], insert=[0, 1.2]
[ok ] real verify() agrees with Factorio inserter flow (probe, all dirs)
[ok ] entity footprints match the SIZE table — checked assembling-machine-2, chemical-plant, electric-energy-interface, electric-furnace, infinity-chest, infinity-pipe, inserter, loader, long-handed-inserter, pipe, pipe-to-ground, splitter, steel-chest, storage-tank, substation, transport-belt, underground-belt
[ok ] pipe-to-ground tunnels opposite its open mouth — open dir=0, underground dir=8
[ok ] chemical-plant fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((0, -1), 'input'), ((0, 3), 'output'), ((2, -1), 'input'), ((2, 3), 'output')]
[ok ] assembling-machine-2 fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((1, -1), 'input'), ((1, 3), 'output')]

Showcase: 5/5 shown cases verify · full corpus and generator head-to-head in STATUS.md · source on GitHub · renders by FBSR (real game sprites).
